description: An exterior view of an apartment building at night. A rooster crows loudly.
The setting changes to a dimly lit living room. The rooster continues to crow.
Rachel walks into the living room wearing pajamas, her hair slightly messy. The rooster crows again.
Rachel gestures with her hands and says, "What the hell is that?"
Monica walks into the living room behind Rachel, also wearing pajamas. The rooster continues to crow.
Rachel turns toward Monica and asks, "What the hell is that? Is that you?"
Monica rubs her face and eyes with her hands as she walks past Rachel.
"Oh," Monica groans audibly.
Rachel points at Monica as she passes by.
Rachel says, "Boy, you are really not a morning person." Monica pushes past Rachel's outstretched arm and replies, "Back off."
Rachel and Monica walk out of the apartment and into a brightly lit hallway, approaching a door across the hall.
Rachel repeatedly bangs her fists against the door and says, "Get up. Get up. Get up."
"Get up," Rachel says, continuing to pound on the door.
Rachel starts to say, "Get" as the door opens, revealing Joey and Chandler standing in pajamas and robes.
Joey and Chandler stand in the doorway looking toward Rachel and Monica.
Rachel puts her hands on her hips and asks, "What is that noise?" Chandler replies, "You," maintaining a static expression.
Joey rubs his right eye.
"It's the chick," Joey says.
Joey continues, "She's going through some changes."
Rachel and Monica look at Joey with their hands on their hips.
"What kind of changes?" Rachel asks. Chandler answers, "Well, the vet seems to think that she's becoming a rooster," making a brief hand gesture.
Rachel and Monica stand still, gazing steadily at Chandler and Joey.
Chandler gestures slightly with one hand and says, "We're getting a second opinion."
The setting changes to a black screen.
The screen remains black. A brief instrumental music cue plays.

An exterior daytime view of the city skyline is shown. The setting then changes to a brightly lit apartment kitchen where Ross, Phoebe, and Chandler sit around a table. Rachel enters holding a shopping bag.
"Hi," Rachel says. "Hey," Phoebe says. Chandler looks up and asks, "What are you doing shopping at eight in the morning?"
"Well, I've been up since six, thanks to somebody's dumb ass rooster," Rachel replies, setting her bag on the counter.
Phoebe sits at the table, looking toward Chandler.
"You guys, you really should get rid of those animals. They shouldn't be living in an apartment," Phoebe says. Chandler adds, "Yeah,"
"especially not with all these knives and cookbooks around," Chandler finishes, looking down at his newspaper.
Phoebe stands up from the table and picks up a red coat.
Phoebe puts on her coat and says, "All right, I'm going to go to the fertility doctor and see if I'm ready to have Frank and Alice's embryo transferred into my uterus."
Ross, holding a mug, looks up at Phoebe.
"Now, how will they know if you're ready?" Ross asks.
Phoebe answers, "Oh, they're just going to look and see if my endometrial layer is thick."
Chandler begins to speak, saying, "Oh, I"
"can check that for you," Chandler says, looking up at Phoebe.
Phoebe briefly looks at Chandler.
"Okay, everyone think thick," Phoebe says with a smile.
"Good luck," Ross says. "Good luck," Chandler echoes as Phoebe walks toward the door.
Phoebe opens the door. Monica stands just outside holding a laundry basket, with Joey behind her.
"Hey," Monica says. "Wish me luck," Phoebe says as she walks past Monica.
"Oh, good luck," Monica replies. Joey steps inside and says, "Good luck. And I'm still right."
Monica turns toward Joey and says, "So not true."
"What?" Chandler asks from the table.
Joey points at Monica and says, "She's mad because I know today's her laundry day and that means she's wearing her old lady underpants."
Monica stands still, holding her laundry basket and looking at Joey.
"I can check that for you," Chandler says from the table.
Monica glares at Chandler, then turns her gaze back to Joey.
"I just can't believe that you think you and Chandler know me and Rachel better than we know you," Monica says to Joey.
Chandler, now standing next to Joey, says, "Well, we do."
Chandler looks at Monica.
Chandler points at Monica and says, "You can only eat Tic Tacs in even numbers."
Joey looks at Monica.
"Yeah, what's that about?" Joey asks.
Chandler walks toward Rachel.
Pointing at Rachel, Chandler says, "And you."
Chandler turns to Ross and says, "Ross, I believe if you check Rachel's bag you will find a half-eaten box of cookies in there."
Ross stands up, reaches into Rachel's shopping bag, and pulls out a box of cookies. He removes a partially eaten cookie from the box.
Holding up the cookie, Ross says, "You're good."
Ross takes a bite of the cookie. Rachel stands with her arms crossed, looking toward Chandler.
Rachel says, "These are not," maintaining her crossed arms as the segment ends.

In a living area and kitchen, Rachel stands with her arms crossed. Joey turns to Ross, who is looking into a paper shopping bag on the counter. Rachel says, "So not impressed," and Joey replies, "Everybody snacks when they shop."
Chandler adds, "Yeah. Oh, yeah." Joey then turns to Ross, pointing, and asks, "Ross, how many items left in that bag?"
Ross leans toward the bag and says, "Five."
Chandler gestures to Rachel and Monica, saying, "Okay, $10 says that we can name every item in that bag."
Rachel slightly unfolds her arms.
Rachel asks, "How many guesses do you get?"
Joey tilts his head.
Joey says, "Six," holding up a hand. Monica walks into the kitchen, extends her hand toward Joey, and says, "Challenge extended."
Joey turns to face Monica.
Joey points at Monica and says, "Deal." Monica shakes his hand, saying, "Challenge accepted." Ross moves the paper bag onto the kitchen table.
Joey claps his hands together and rubs them.
Joey raises his hands, palms open, and says, "All right, we'll start with apples."
Ross pulls the bag closer, gripping the handles, and repeats, "We'll be starting with apples," his pitch higher.
Chandler stares at Ross with a flat expression as Ross holds his hands over the bag.
Chandler points at Ross and says, "Stop that now."
Ross presses his lips together and reaches into the bag.
Ross pulls out an item and says, "Yes."
Ross places a bag of apples on the table.
Joey says, "Okay."
Chandler says, "Tortilla chips."
Joey says, "Yogurt," as Ross pulls out a bag of chips. Chandler says, "Diet soda." Ross quickly pulls out a container and a pack of cans, saying, "Yes" repeatedly.
Rachel looks at Monica, then turns back toward the men.
Rachel points and asks, "Orange juice?" She then raises her arms in the air, smiling, and shouts, "No, there's no orange juice in there. We win."
Ross holds his hands up and says, "They have another guess."
Rachel lowers her arms.
Rachel crosses her arms and says, "Okay, well, we won that one."
Joey claps his hands together.
Joey rubs his hands together and says, "Okay, the last thing."
Chandler leans in and whispers into Joey's ear. Joey turns his head slightly, listening.
Joey shakes his head, gesturing with his hands, and replies, "No, not for like another two weeks."
Rachel and Monica watch them with crossed arms.
Chandler raises one finger and says, "I got it."
Chandler points forward and says, "Scotch tape."
Ross reaches into the paper bag and pulls out a roll of tape, holding it up.
Joey and Chandler laugh and bump their fists together, while Rachel and Monica watch with widened eyes.
Rachel looks at Chandler and asks, "How did you know she would buy Scotch tape?" Chandler smiles and replies, "Well, we used up theirs last night making scary faces."
Chandler and Joey laugh, bumping fists again.
Chandler laughs, saying, "Oh, man. All right, $10," as Joey gestures with his hands.
Chandler points at Rachel and Monica, saying, "Fork it over. Cough it up. Pay the piper."
Chandler keeps his hand extended.
Chandler says, "Give me it."
Rachel looks at Monica.
Monica shakes her head and says, "That does not mean you know us better."
Rachel points at Joey and says, "I want a rematch." Monica adds, "Yeah, and none of these stupid grocery questions."
Rachel says, "Real personal questions," and Monica follows with, "Yeah, and the winner gets"
Monica says, "a," then Joey asks, "Serious?" Monica then asks, "Are you scared?"
Joey shakes his head slightly.
Joey says, "No."
Chandler looks toward Ross.
Joey gestures toward Monica and Rachel, asking, "Who makes up the questions?" Chandler points his thumb at Ross and says, "Ross will do it."
Ross spreads his arms and says, "Oh, sure, Ross will do it. Sound like he has a job or a child or a life of his own."
Monica turns her head slightly and replies, "Fine, we'll"
Monica completes her sentence, saying, "ask." Ross shakes his head and says, "No, no. I want to play."
The setting changes to a doctor's examination room. Dr. Zane looks at an ultrasound monitor. Phoebe sits on the exam table in a hospital gown. Frank Jr. and Alice stand next to her.
Dr. Zane turns to Phoebe and says, "Looks like your uterus is ready for implantation."
Phoebe says, "Oh, I knew it. I knew it. I felt really sick this morning," raising her fists briefly and smiling.
Frank Jr. claps his hands together.
Frank Jr. gestures with his hands, saying, "Well, okay. So what's now? Go get the eggs. Put them in there."
Dr. Zane replies, "Okay. It'll take just a little while to prepare the embryos."
Phoebe tilts her head and asks, "Embryos? As in more than one?" Dr. Zane answers, "Five, actually." Phoebe repeats, "Five?"
Phoebe gestures with her hands and asks, "Okay, where am I giving birth? In a hospital or a big box under the stairs?"
Dr. Zane smiles and places a hand on Phoebe's shoulder.
Dr. Zane says, "We do five because that gives you a 25% chance that at least one will attach."
Phoebe asks, "That's it?"
Phoebe continues, "25%? That means that it's, like, 75% chance of no baby at all." Dr. Zane nods and says, "Yeah."
Frank Jr. touches his hands together and says, "You know, I was thinking, what are the odds like if"
Frank Jr. makes a stuffing motion with his hands as he finishes, "you stuffed, like, 200 of them in there?"
Alice gently touches Frank Jr.'s arm.
Alice says, "Sweetie, now she's a woman, not a gumball machine."
Frank Jr. looks down with a slightly furrowed brow as the segment ends.

In a clinic room, Phoebe sits on an exam table wearing a patterned hospital gown. Frank Jr. and Alice stand beside her, while Dr. Zane stands nearby with her back turned. Phoebe gestures and says, "Okay. Well, you know what? Don't worry, you guys. Because I'm going to do this as many times as it takes to get it right. Well,"
Frank Jr. holds his hands out, preparing to speak.
Frank Jr. gestures with his hands and says, "See, the thing is, we kind of only have one shot."
Alice looks toward Phoebe.
Alice says, "It costs $16,000 each time you do this." Phoebe's eyes widen and her jaw drops as she repeats, "$16,000?" Dr. Zane looks over her shoulder.
Dr. Zane turns to face the group.
Dr. Zane says, "Look, it's 20 in California."
Alice holds Frank Jr.'s hand and gestures as she prepares to speak.
Alice says, "We're kind of using all the money we have just to do it this one time."
Phoebe says, "Whoa."
Phoebe looks away momentarily, her mouth open slightly.
Phoebe says, "Okay, that's a lot of pressure on me and my uterus."
Phoebe turns her head toward Dr. Zane, who approaches the exam table.
Phoebe gestures with both hands toward Dr. Zane and says, "Okay, so maybe there's something that I can do just to help make sure I get pregnant."
Dr. Zane shakes her head slightly and says, "No. I'm sorry."
Phoebe lowers her hands and says, "Wow, you guys really don't know anything."
Dr. Zane holds a green folder and looks at Phoebe, while Frank Jr. points a finger upward.
Frank Jr. gestures with his hand and says, "I know. Why don't you get drunk?"
Frank Jr. continues, "That worked for a bunch of girls in my high school."
Dr. Zane looks at Frank Jr. with her mouth slightly open.
The setting changes to a snowy city street outside a building. A brief vocal singing "You." is audible.
Now in Monica and Rachel's apartment, Ross kneels while holding the edge of a large board. Monica walks into the room and approaches him.
Monica touches the top of the board and asks, "How's the test coming?" Ross pulls the board back slightly and says, "Hey, you're touching the game board."
Monica retracts her hand.
Monica says, "Sorry."
Monica turns and walks away from Ross.
Monica says, "You freak," as she walks.
Chandler, Rachel, and Joey sit around the dining table. Monica approaches them and gestures with her hand.
Monica stands by the table and says, "Hey, guys, do you realize that any minute now Phoebe could be pregnant?"
Rachel leans forward slightly and says, "I know. It's such a huge, life altering thing."
Monica says, "I know."
Ross steps away from the board, which is now set up on a stand.
Ross calls out loudly, "The test is ready."
Chandler, Rachel, and Joey abruptly stand up from the table. They rush over to the living room couch, cheering audibly.
Ross says, "Okay," as Joey and Chandler sit on the left side of the couch, and Rachel and Monica sit on the right.
Ross stands near the board, gesturing as he says, "Each team will answer ten questions. The first team to answer the most questions wins. The categories"
Ross turns and points to the board.
Ross points to different columns on the board and says, "are fears and pet peeves, ancient history, literature, and"
Ross points to the last column and finishes, "it's all relative."
The four on the couch watch Ross as he reaches into his pocket.
Ross pulls out a coin and says, "Now the coin toss to see who goes first." He tosses the coin into the air.
The coin lands on the floor. Joey, Chandler, Rachel, and Monica lean forward with their hands clasped.
Ross bends down to retrieve the coin from the floor.
Ross stands up and says, "Okay,"
Ross holds the coin in his hand.
Ross says, "Somebody call it. This time"
Rachel points her finger upward, her mouth moving as she shouts. Ross looks at the coin in his hand.
Ross says, "It's heads."
Joey and Chandler shout loudly and pump their fists, while Rachel and Monica lower their arms and heads.
Ross points to the men with a marker and says, "Gentlemen, pick your category."
Chandler answers, "Fears and pet peeves."
Ross removes a card from the board.
Ross reads, "What is Monica's biggest pet peeve?" Joey answers rapidly, "Animals dressed as humans." Ross points at him and confirms, "That's correct."
Rachel and Monica point at each other and speak inaudibly. Ross uses a marker to cross out a square on the board.
Ross turns to the women and says, "Ladies, same category."
Ross pulls a card from the board and looks at it.
Ross reads, "According to Chandler, what phenomenon scares the bejesus out of him?"
Monica's eyes widen.
Monica leans forward quickly and exclaims loudly, "Michael Flatley, Lord of the Dance."
Ross points toward Monica with the marker.
Ross confirms, "That is correct."
Rachel and Monica grasp each other's hands and cheer loudly.
Joey furrows his brow, turns to Chandler, and says, "The Irish jig guy."
Chandler turns to Joey.
Chandler speaks loudly, swinging his arms as he says, "His legs flail about as if independent from his body."
Joey leans back, his mouth slightly open.
Ross addresses the men, saying, "Gentlemen, your pick."
Joey points forward and says, "It's all relative."
Ross pulls another card from the board.
Ross reads from the card, "Monica and I had a grandmother who died. You both went to her funeral. Name that grandmother."
Joey presses his fist to his mouth.
Joey removes his fist and says, "Nana."
Ross replies, "She has a real name."
Chandler gestures with his hands, his brow furrowed.
Chandler shouts, "Althea." Joey points at him, repeating, "Althea. What are you doing?" Chandler answers, "I took a shot."
Joey responds, "You're shooting with Althea." Ross confirms, "Althea is correct." Chandler adds, "Nice shooting."
Joey stares at Chandler with his mouth slightly open as the segment ends.
In an apartment living room, Ross stands holding a yellow card and a marker. Joey and Chandler sit on a sofa to his left, while Rachel and Monica sit on a coffee table next to them.
Rachel pumps her fists and says, "We'll take literature."
Ross reads from the yellow card, "Every week the TV Guide comes to Chandler and Joey's apartment."
Ross continues reading, "What name appears on the"
Rachel stands up, pointing forward, and says, "address label? Chandler."
Rachel says, "Guess it. It's Chandler Bing." Monica shouts, "No."
Ross looks at the card and says, "I'm afraid the TV Guide comes to Chanandler Bong."
Joey and Chandler laugh, bringing their closed hands together in a double fist bump.
Monica turns her head toward Rachel and yells, "I knew that. Rachel, use your head." Rachel lowers her head.
Rachel sits back down on the coffee table.
Chandler leans forward, pointing a finger, and says, "Actually, it's Miss Chanandler Bong."
The women sit in silence. The setting changes to a medical room. A small petri dish rests on a blue towel atop an examination table.
Phoebe, wearing a yellow hospital gown, leans closely over the petri dish.
Looking at the dish, Phoebe says, "Hello, teeny."
Phoebe remains leaning over the dish, her gaze fixed on it.
Phoebe says, "Well, I'm Phoebe Buffay."
Phoebe smiles slightly, her lips parting as she adds, "Hi."
Phoebe continues looking steadily at the petri dish.
Phoebe says, "I'm hoping to be your uterus for the next nine months."
Phoebe shifts her gaze slightly upward.
Phoebe looks back at the dish and says, "You should know that we're doing this for Frank and Alice. Who? You know you've been"
Phoebe gestures lightly with her hand while maintaining her gaze on the dish.
Phoebe brings her hands to her chest, her fingers spread, and says, "know they want you so much. So when you guys get in there, really grab on,"
Phoebe keeps her hands on her chest.
Phoebe says, "okay? And I promise that I'll keep you safe and warm"
Phoebe gestures outward with one hand and continues, "until you're ready to have them take you home,"
Phoebe continues gesturing outward.
Phoebe says, "so"
Phoebe rests her hands on the edge of the examination table.
Phoebe raises both hands in a brief wave and says, "Oh, and also, next time you see me, if I'm screaming, don't worry."
Phoebe lowers her hands and adds, "That's what's supposed to happen."
The setting changes to a wider view of the medical room as a female doctor enters through a door. Phoebe sits up straight.
The doctor asks, "Ready?"
Phoebe smiles and nods at the doctor.
Phoebe looks down at the petri dish and says, "Good luck."
The setting changes back to the apartment living room. Ross stands near a whiteboard displaying categories written in black marker.
Ross gestures with his hands and says, "All right, the score is nine to eight in favor of the guys."
Ross points toward the women, saying, "Ladies, if you miss this, the game is theirs. Pick your category." Rachel stands up, clenching her fists, and yells, "It's all relative."
Ross holds up a finger toward Rachel.
Ross says, "You don't have to shout everything." Rachel lowers her arms and replies, "I'm sorry," her volume softer than her previous line.
Ross turns to the whiteboard, pulls a purple card from it, and holds it in front of him.
Ross reads from the purple card, "What is the name of Chandler's father's?"
Ross continues reading, "Las Vegas all-male burlesque."
Monica stands up, her arms bent with clenched fists, and shouts, "Viva Las Gaygas."
Chandler and Joey look downward, their shoulders slumped.
Chandler looks up and says, "Unfortunately, that is correct."
Rachel and Monica jump repeatedly, cheer loudly, and embrace each other.
Ross claps his hands together and says, "All right, we have a tie."
Ross reaches into his pants pocket, saying, "Luckily, I have prepared for such an event."
Ross pulls a small white card with a yellow lightning bolt drawn on it from his pocket.
Ross holds the card up with both hands in front of his chest and announces loudly, "The lightning round."
Joey and Chandler stare forward with wide eyes and open mouths, while Rachel and Monica jump repeatedly.
Ross holds the card up, pointing a finger, and says, "30 seconds. All the questions you can answer."
Monica points a finger toward the men.
Monica shouts, "You guys are dead. I am so good at lightning rounds." Joey gestures with his hands in response.
The screen turns black.
The screen remains black until the segment ends.
